name: charity: water
mission: charity: water is a nonprofit organization bringing clean and safe drinking water to people in developing countries. The organization inspires giving and empowers others to fundraise for sustainable water solutions. 100% of public donations are sent to local partners on the ground, who build and implement the projects. When the projects are completed, charity: water proves every one of them using GPS coordinates, photos and details of the community served.
theoryOfChange: Charity Water utilizes a 'MAP Framework' which is a monitoring and evaluation framework designed to collect data across various water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) indicators. This framework helps them understand the impact of funded projects, maximize effectiveness, and guide future funding decisions. The MAP Framework includes two types of data collection: Annual Check-ins (household surveys at baseline and endline of a grant) and Post-Implementation Monitoring (a comprehensive assessment of past work every few years). The framework aims to answer questions about immediate improvements, sustained access, and reasons for water point functionality issues.

Are comfortable with their donation being used for general humanitarian relief

Focus is on long-term infrastructure rather than immediate emergency aid
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors specifically looking to fulfill their religious zakat obligation or those seeking a high program-to-overhead ratio above 90%.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The charity tracks <cite id="17">three key outcome metrics</cite> and has a documented theory of change.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The charity uses a community-led model to install water points, reducing waterborne illness and freeing up time for education and work.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking outcomes ensures that wells remain functional and actually improve health metrics over time.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The global water crisis requires billions in investment; the charity has the infrastructure to scale.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains an <cite id="5">overall score of 100.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting the highest standards of transparency.
They spend only <cite id="6">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite>, which is significantly better than many large-scale humanitarian peers.
The charity holds <cite id="7">10.1 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring program stability even during economic downturns.

strengths_deep_dive: The organization's financial model is built on extreme transparency. They have earned a <cite id="8">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level available. This is supported by a <cite id="9">91% independent board</cite>, ensuring strong oversight of executive decisions and financial management. Furthermore, their <cite id="10">fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised</cite> demonstrates a lean operation that maximizes the resources available for its core mission of water provision.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2006, charity: water is a global non-profit dedicated to solving the water crisis through sustainable, community-led solutions. The organization operates with a unique model that separates its operational costs from its water project funding. They have successfully <cite id="1">funded 9,395 water projects</cite> according to recent filings, reaching millions across Asia and Africa. The charity reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $96,818,300</cite> in its most recent fiscal cycle, maintaining a massive scale of operations while focusing on high-impact interventions. Their programs include <cite id="3">drilled wells, spring protections, and BioSand Filters</cite>, which are tailored to the specific needs of each local environment. By partnering with local organizations, they ensure that water points are maintained for the long term. This focus on sustainability is paired with <cite id="4">exceptional financial health</cite>, as evidenced by their perfect accountability ratings. While the charity does not facilitate zakat, it offers a compelling option for sadaqah, particularly for donors who value high-transparency models and measurable humanitarian outcomes in underserved regions.
